pattern: The full lap is only ever entered and exited at its two named endpoints, so instead of stepping through every sector one visit at a time, you can describe every sector's visit count just from where the first round begins and where the last round ends.
hints: Every round after the first, the amount you gain in the middle rounds does not matter for finding the busiest sector -- watch what happens to a sector that is neither the very first start nor the very last end.

Every sector between the earliest starting point and the final ending point gets crossed an equal number of extra times, except for exactly one contiguous arc which gets crossed one more time than the rest.

That extra-visited arc runs directly from the very first sector of round one through to the very last sector of the final round, wrapping past the top of the numbering back to 1 if the finish comes before the start; simply reporting that arc, in ascending numeric order, is the whole answer.

edge_inputs.description: maximum sector count, single round wrapping across the seam
several rounds crossing the 1/n seam in mixed directions
bouncing between two adjacent sectors many times to build a clear tie
start above end so the visited arc wraps through sector 1
many short rounds all moving forward one sector at a time

stress.description: random legal round sequences (no two consecutive stops equal) across the full n range
stress.note: consecutive rounds entries are always kept distinct, matching how a marathon leg must actually move

complexity.time: O(n) to build the answer arc
